channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Pope Leo XIV rejects claims that God justifies war in Palm Sunday Mass message
item.description: Pope Leo XIV rejected claims that God justifies war and prayed especially for Christians in the Middle East during a Palm Sunday Mass before tens of thousands of people in St. Peter's Square.
item.content:encoded:
Pope Leo XIV rejected claims that God justifies war and prayed especially for Christians in the Middle East during a Palm Sunday Mass before tens of thousands of people in St. Peter's Square.

(Image credit: Remo Casilli)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: How to navigate the maze of drug discounts to get the best price
item.description: In February, TrumpRx joined a growing list of websites consumers can tap for discounts on their medicines. Here's a cheat sheet for getting the best deal.
item.content:encoded: In February, TrumpRx joined a growing list of websites consumers can tap for discounts on their medicines. Here's a cheat sheet for getting the best deal.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Lupkin

item.title: Pakistan hosts diplomatic discussions on ending war
item.description: Foreign ministers from Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkey and Egypt will meet in Islamabad today in an attempt to come up with a plan to de-escalate the Iran war, after another group got involved in the expanding conflict: Yemen's Houthi rebels.
item.content:encoded:
Foreign ministers from Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkey and Egypt will meet in Islamabad today in an attempt to come up with a plan to de-escalate the Iran war, after another group got involved in the expanding conflict: Yemen's Houthi rebels.

(Image credit: Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Why a 98-year-old federal judge is asking the Supreme Court for her job back
item.description: Pauline Newman's story shines a light on the aging judiciary, where judges are getting older and lifetime tenure is raising thorny questions about retirement.
item.content:encoded:
Pauline Newman's story shines a light on the aging judiciary, where judges are getting older and lifetime tenure is raising thorny questions about retirement.

(Image credit: Paul J. Richards)
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson

item.title: Some critics of birthright citizenship say it's a fraud issue. What does that mean?
item.description: Advocates for ending birthright citizenship point to "birth tourism" schemes to argue that the legal principle is ripe for exploitation and threatens national security. Experts say it's not so simple.
item.content:encoded:
Advocates for ending birthright citizenship point to "birth tourism" schemes to argue that the legal principle is ripe for exploitation and threatens national security. Experts say it's not so simple.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: He wants children's bikes made in the U.S.A. — and tariffs against his rivals
item.description: Nearly all the bicycles sold in the United States are made overseas. An Indiana company set out to change that — and it's seeking a push from the Trump administration's tariffs.
item.content:encoded:
Nearly all the bicycles sold in the United States are made overseas. An Indiana company set out to change that — and it's seeking a push from the Trump administration's tariffs.

(Image credit: Scott Horsley)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Photos: 'No Kings' protests across the country
item.description: People showed up for rallies in more than 3,000 communities from coast to coast on Saturday, to vent their frustration and decry the policies of the Trump administration.
item.content:encoded:
People showed up for rallies in more than 3,000 communities from coast to coast on Saturday, to vent their frustration and decry the policies of the Trump administration.

(Image credit: KEN CEDENO/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Meredith Nierman

item.title: Opinion: White House 'gamifying' Iran war updates
item.description: The White House has depicted the war in Iran online with videos that weave real life images of missile strikes and destruction with clips from video games, sports clips, and action movies.
item.content:encoded:
The White House has depicted the war in Iran online with videos that weave real life images of missile strikes and destruction with clips from video games, sports clips, and action movies.

(Image credit: White House via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: Ranking Member of House Armed Services Committee Adam Smith discusses the war on Iran
item.description: NPR's Scott Simon talks with House Armed Service Committee ranking member Adam Smith, D-Wash., about the war on Iran, now a month old, and DHS funding.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Simon talks with House Armed Service Committee ranking member Adam Smith, D-Wash., about the war on Iran, now a month old, and DHS funding.
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: There's a massive measles vaccine campaign in Mexico. Is the public on board?
item.description: With tens of thousands of suspected cases, the government is aiming for 2.5 million jabs a week. The response has been encouraging — but also worrisome.
item.content:encoded:
With tens of thousands of suspected cases, the government is aiming for 2.5 million jabs a week. The response has been encouraging — but also worrisome.

(Image credit: Gerardo Vieyra/NurPhoto)
item.dc:creator: Katie Silver
